text:
Your sensors flag something that shouldn't be here — a pocket of spacetime where the readings fold back on themselves. Not dangerous at this distance, but deeply wrong by every model your navigation computer knows.

The anomaly pulses. Slowly. Like breathing.

Your instruments can measure it. They can't explain it.

choices.label: Move closer and take detailed readings
choices.tone_note: Curiosity. The thing that drives explorers into the dark.

effects.description: Investigated a spacetime anomaly. Readings suggest something artificial — a structure or device operating on principles outside known physics.

effects.text: Moved in close. The readings will take time to process, but something here is worth understanding.

choices.label: Log the coordinates and keep your distance

effects.description: Logged an anomalous spacetime reading. Coordinates recorded for later investigation.

effects.text: Logged it. The coordinates aren't going anywhere.

choices.label: Mark it as a navigation hazard and move on
choices.tone_note: Pragmatic. Not everything is your problem.

effects.text: Filed the anomaly as a navigation hazard. Someone else can be curious.

text:
The surface survey picks up geometric patterns beneath the topsoil — too precise for geology, too old for anything in the colonial record. Whatever built this was here long before anyone claimed this world.

Excavation would take days. A surface scan gives you outlines: chambers, corridors, something that might be a central structure. No power signatures. No atmosphere. Just stone and silence and the weight of years.

choices.label: Send a team to the central structure

effects.description: Explored surface ruins. The central structure contained inscriptions in an unknown script and a chamber that may have served a ceremonial or scientific purpose.

effects.text: The team returned with scans and samples. More questions than answers, but that's how it starts.

choices.label: Conduct a thorough surface scan from orbit

effects.description: Mapped surface ruins from orbit. Structure predates known colonization by centuries. Full excavation would require ground teams.

effects.text: Orbital scans complete. The ruins are older than expected. Someone will want this data.

choices.label: Note the location and move on
choices.tone_note: Not every mystery is yours to solve.

effects.text: Logged the coordinates. The ruins have waited this long — they can wait longer.

text:
Your sensor sweep catches something at the edge of resolution — a faint, repeating signal buried in the system's background noise. It's not a natural emission. The pattern is too regular, too structured.

It could be a beacon. It could be an echo of something that stopped transmitting years ago. Your sensors can't tell from here — you'd need to get closer, or allocate processing time to clean up the signal.

choices.label: Allocate processing time to analyze the signal

effects.description: Detected a faint structured signal during system scan. Analysis suggests an artificial source, possibly a dormant beacon or relay.

effects.text: The analysis will take time, but the signal is real. Something is out there.

choices.label: Log the bearing for later investigation

effects.text: Logged the signal bearing. If it's still transmitting next time, it'll be easier to find.

text:
Nothing here but starlight and the faint hiss of cosmic background radiation on your sensors. The system is quiet — the kind of quiet that has weight to it.

Your navigator marks the position on the chart. Another point of reference in the vastness.

choices.label: Run a passive scan while you're here

effects.text: Ran a passive scan. Nothing unusual — but the silence was noted.
